input.title: Project Management Tool
input.description: Project Management Tool is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, MongoDB for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Project Creation and Setup
requirements: Create project with name, description, and team members
Set project start and end dates
Choose project methodology (Agile, Kanban, Waterfall)
Upload project documents and resources

FR-02.title: Task Management
requirements: Create tasks with title, description, assignee, and due date
Set task priority (low, medium, high, critical)
Organize tasks into columns (To Do, In Progress, Done)
Drag-and-drop task cards between columns

FR-03.title: Sprint Planning
requirements: Create sprints with start/end dates and goals
Add tasks to sprints from backlog
Sprint burndown chart showing progress
Sprint review with velocity metrics

FR-04.title: Team Collaboration
requirements: Comment on tasks with mentions
File attachments on tasks
Activity log showing all changes
Real-time updates when team members make changes

FR-05.title: Time Tracking
requirements: Log time spent on tasks
Start/stop timer for active work
Weekly timesheet view
Time reports per project and team member

FR-06.title: Gantt Chart View
requirements: Visual timeline of tasks and milestones
Task dependencies shown as connecting lines
Drag to adjust task dates
Critical path highlighting

FR-07.title: Reporting Dashboard
requirements: Project status overview with completion percentage
Team workload distribution chart
Overdue tasks report
Custom report builder with filters

FR-08.title: Role-Based Access Control
requirements: Admin, Manager, and Member roles
Admins can manage all projects and settings
Managers can create and assign tasks
Members can view and update their assigned tasks

FR-09.title: Integration with External Tools
requirements: Integrate with GitHub for commit linking
Slack notifications for task updates
Calendar sync for due dates
Webhook support for custom integrations

FR-10.title: Template System
requirements: Save project as template
Create project from existing template
Pre-built templates for common project types
Templates include task structure and settings

FR-01.title: Plan Project Creation and Setup Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for project creation and setup and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Create project with name, description, and team members; Set project start and end dates; Choose project methodology (Agile, Kanban, Waterfall).
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for project creation and setup is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for project creation and setup are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for project creation and setup can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Project Creation and Setup Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for project creation and setup on React, Node.js, MongoDB. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Create project with name, description, and team members; Set project start and end dates; Choose project methodology (Agile, Kanban, Waterfall).
acceptance_criteria: Core project creation and setup business rules are implemented for Project Management Tool
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for project creation and setup
API/service behavior for project creation and setup is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Project Creation and Setup Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for project creation and setup with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The project creation and setup user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for project creation and setup behave correctly
The project creation and setup UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Project Creation and Setup Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for project creation and setup. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for project creation and setup supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for project creation and setup are defined correctly
Migrations for project creation and setup can be applied safely in target environments

FR-01.title: Write Project Creation and Setup Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for project creation and setup. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for project creation and setup are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for project creation and setup are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical project creation and setup scenarios

Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Save project as template; Create project from existing template; Pre-built templates for common project types.
acceptance_criteria: Core template system business rules are implemented for Project Management Tool
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for template system
API/service behavior for template system is testable and documented for integration

FR-10.title: Implement Template System Frontend Flow
FR-10.description: Build the frontend flow for template system with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The template system user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for template system behave correctly
The template system UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-10.title: Design Template System Persistence Model
FR-10.description: Design the persistence layer for template system. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for template system supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for template system are defined correctly
Migrations for template system can be applied safely in target environments

FR-10.title: Write Template System Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-10.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for template system. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for template system are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for template system are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical template system scenarios
